creator: koobs@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Jean-Sébastien Pédron from comment #5)

Ah! In that case any ports committer may give you approval (>0).

Though just to be clear, ports committers are technically only approving the 'commit' proposal and the change syntactically, rather than the software specific change semantics.

If the change in D3491 passes poudriere (there's no mention of QA results/logs in the review), you are clear to commit
